steps.observed: App loaded, NeuroLang Sparklis title visible with engine selector and predicate browser

steps.expected: Engine selected, predicates and query editor become available
steps.observed: neurosynth selected (highlighted), predicates listed, Run Query button appeared

steps.action: Click code editor, select all (Meta+A), then type the Neurosynth CBMA query with ActivationGivenTermImage(agg_create_region_overlay(...))
steps.expected: Query entered in CodeMirror editor with 5 lines
steps.observed: Query entered successfully, Run Query button enabled, Out of sync indicator visible

steps.expected: Query executes, loading indicator shown, then results appear
steps.observed: Query completed immediately (cached/fast); results appeared showing ActivationGivenTermImage symbol selected, 1 row with agg_create_region_overlay and VBROverlay badge

steps.expected: VBROverlay row visible with 'View in brain' button
steps.observed: ActivationGivenTermImage already selected; 1 row showing agg_create_region_overlay with VBROverlay badge; 'View in brain' button (ref=e68) visible

steps.expected: Overlay loads in Niivue viewer, color bar appears with probability scale
steps.observed: Overlay loaded; OVERLAYS (1) panel shows 'ActivationGivenTermImage [0]' with orange swatch; color bar visible with 0 to 1 probability scale using 'hot' color map (black to white gradient)
Both elements confirmed via JS eval: colorBar='found', colorBarGradient='found'; color bar text shows '0' and '1' labels; gradient style: linear-gradient hot colormap
No errors. Warnings only: WebGL performance (GPU stall ReadPixels - benign), and '98% of voxels are zero: ignoring zeros for cal_max' (expected for sparse CBMA probability map)

evidence.consoleErrors: none (warnings only: WebGL ReadPixels performance, 98% voxels zero)

frictions.description: The brain viewer and color bar are below the fold and require scrolling the main-content div (not the window) to be visible in screenshots. The main-content has overflowY:auto and the brain panel starts at y=721px, color bar at y=1187px in a 900px viewport.
frictions.resolution: Used JS eval to scroll the .main-content div to position 600 to bring color bar into view for screenshots

frictions.description: The Niivue canvas shows the brain atlas (grey slices) but the probability overlay activation is not prominently visible in the canvas at the default crosshair position (0,0,0). This is expected because 98% of voxels are zero (sparse CBMA map for 'emotion' term).
frictions.resolution: Confirmed via DOM inspection that the overlay IS loaded (OVERLAYS (1) panel, color bar with 0-1 scale). The visual sparseness is correct behavior for CBMA.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: VAL-BRAIN-003 PASS. Neurosynth CBMA query with ActivationGivenTermImage(agg_create_region_overlay) completed successfully. Clicking 'View in brain' loaded an ExplicitVBROverlay into Niivue with the overlay manager showing 1 overlay and a color bar with 'hot' colormap displaying probability scale from 0 to 1. All required DOM elements confirmed: data-testid='color-bar', data-testid='color-bar-gradient', data-testid='overlay-manager'. No console errors.
